jobs.title: Member of Technical Staff
jobs.descriptionHtml:
About Anterior

Anterior is on a mission to transform healthcare administration, making it seamless and invisible so clinicians can focus on delivering care. We’ve built an AI-powered platform designed by clinicians, for clinicians, to simplify administrative workflows and improve patient outcomes. By combining clinical expertise with cutting-edge technology, we’re revolutionizing healthcare operations with responsible AI.

We're growing rapidly and are backed by world-class investors including Sequoia, New Enterprise Associates (NEA), and Neo along with notable angels including the founders of DeepMind, Google AI, and Inflection AI.

Anterior is improving the experience of healthcare for everyone. We're working on the cutting edge of using technology to make clinical decision-making frictionless.

We're building exciting tech for huge customers and we trust in the foundations: keep it simple, build strong foundations, and the rest will follow.

The Team

At Anterior, engineers share a strong "sense of product" and solve meaningful problems that simplify healthcare workflows. We collaborate in multidisciplinary teams to deliver secure, scalable, and user-focused solutions guided by best practices and modular design.

We value diverse experiences and skill sets, continually expanding our team to tackle modern healthcare’s challenges with creativity and precision.

About You

You have a passion for doing things The Right Way, you believe Less is More, you want to focus on the foundations of system design and architecture (pacelc, consistency guarantees), you care as much about the code you do not write, you’re tired of the hype treadmill and care about the fundamentals. You have battle scars from over-engineered and underperforming slop, and you care about writing code that lasts.

Your depth is on the backend and/or infrastructure. You may be the person building APIs, integrations, data pipelines, and distributed systems — or you may be the person scaling and hardening the cloud infrastructure underneath them. Either way, you've owned system design decisions, shipped them to production, and operated them at scale. You may be coming from a scale-up, a big tech platform team, an engineering finance shop, or anywhere with a serious AWS footprint — what matters is that you've seen real complexity and you want more ownership over what you build.

Come share your proudest firefighting story with us in an interview, bring your GitHub, and let’s see if we’re a good fit. If you're a junior passionate about coding and hacking: it's always ok to apply, shoot us a link to some of your work and let's talk anyway.

https://電.anterior.app/ — if you find something here that resonates, mention it in your application.

About the Role

Members of Technical Staff at Anterior own problems end-to-end — from system design through to production. You'll build and scale the core platform and infrastructure that powers clinical decisioning at scale. A day's work may include:

  • Designing and building backend services, APIs, and data pipelines that power our AI platform

  • Scaling and hardening our AWS infrastructure to meet growing enterprise demand

  • Working directly with customers to understand real-world healthcare workflows, scope integrations, and translate requirements into product specs

  • Writing evals to identify weaknesses and drive improvements in our LLM-powered systems

  • Analyzing request logs, debugging event streams, and improving system reliability

  • Working across the stack when needed — from infrastructure to frontend — to ship complete solutions

  • Building internal tooling to automate workflows and increase team leverage

  • Contributing to Anterior's SDKs, developer documentation, and platform architecture

Tech

  • Langs: Python / Go / TS, React (and HTMX if you can help!)

  • Infra: Nix, AWS (currently on CDKTF — actively looking to migrate, bonus points if you've done this before)

What’s in it for you

This is an opportunity to join a company at its major inflection point, using cutting-edge technology to have a meaningful impact in healthcare. You'll work with a world-class team, hold significant responsibility, and have the opportunity to learn and grow rapidly.

We take pride in our strong apprenticeship and coaching culture. We expect our early hires to become leaders both within our company and across the industry. You’ll be given a lot of responsibility and the opportunity for rapid growth.

Benefits:

  • Equity package

  • Competitive, top-of-market salary

  • 100% covered health, dental, and vision insurance

  • Catered lunches and a stocked kitchen

  • Commuter benefits

  • Company laptop along w/ tools you need to succeed

  • Learning & development budget

  • Team-building events

  • Flexible PTO

Anterior is improving the experience of healthcare for everyone. We're working on the cutting edge of using technology to make clinical decision-making frictionless.

We're building exciting tech for huge customers and we trust in the foundations: keep it simple, build strong foundations, and the rest will follow.




THE TEAM

At Anterior, engineers share a strong "sense of product" and solve meaningful problems that simplify healthcare workflows. We collaborate in multidisciplinary teams to deliver secure, scalable, and user-focused solutions guided by best practices and modular design.

We value diverse experiences and skill sets, continually expanding our team to tackle modern healthcare’s challenges with creativity and precision.




ABOUT YOU

You have a passion for doing things The Right Way, you believe Less is More, you want to focus on the foundations of system design and architecture (pacelc, consistency guarantees), you care as much about the code you do not write, you’re tired of the hype treadmill and care about the fundamentals. You have battle scars from over-engineered and underperforming slop, and you care about writing code that lasts.

Your depth is on the backend and/or infrastructure. You may be the person building APIs, integrations, data pipelines, and distributed systems — or you may be the person scaling and hardening the cloud infrastructure underneath them. Either way, you've owned system design decisions, shipped them to production, and operated them at scale. You may be coming from a scale-up, a big tech platform team, an engineering finance shop, or anywhere with a serious AWS footprint — what matters is that you've seen real complexity and you want more ownership over what you build.

Come share your proudest firefighting story with us in an interview, bring your GitHub, and let’s see if we’re a good fit. If you're a junior passionate about coding and hacking: it's always ok to apply, shoot us a link to some of your work and let's talk anyway.

https://電.anterior.app/ https://xn--xp5a.anterior.app — if you find something here that resonates, mention it in your application.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Members of Technical Staff at Anterior own problems end-to-end — from system design through to production. You'll build and scale the core platform and infrastructure that powers clinical decisioning at scale. A day's work may include:

 - Designing and building backend services, APIs, and data pipelines that power our AI platform

 - Scaling and hardening our AWS infrastructure to meet growing enterprise demand

 - Working directly with customers to understand real-world healthcare workflows, scope integrations, and translate requirements into product specs

 - Writing evals to identify weaknesses and drive improvements in our LLM-powered systems

 - Analyzing request logs, debugging event streams, and improving system reliability

 - Working across the stack when needed — from infrastructure to frontend — to ship complete solutions

 - Building internal tooling to automate workflows and increase team leverage

 - Contributing to Anterior's SDKs, developer documentation, and platform architecture




TECH

 - Langs: Python / Go / TS, React (and HTMX if you can help!)

 - Infra: Nix, AWS (currently on CDKTF — actively looking to migrate, bonus points if you've done this before)




WHAT’S IN IT FOR YOU

This is an opportunity to join a company at its major inflection point, using cutting-edge technology to have a meaningful impact in healthcare. You'll work with a world-class team, hold significant responsibility, and have the opportunity to learn and grow rapidly.

We take pride in our strong apprenticeship and coaching culture. We expect our early hires to become leaders both within our company and across the industry. You’ll be given a lot of responsibility and the opportunity for rapid growth.




BENEFITS:

 - Equity package

 - Competitive, top-of-market salary

 - 100% covered health, dental, and vision insurance

 - Catered lunches and a stocked kitchen

 - Commuter benefits

 - Company laptop along w/ tools you need to succeed

 - Learning & development budget

 - Team-building events

 - Flexible PTO

jobs.title: Technical Solutions Architect
jobs.descriptionHtml:
About Anterior:

Anterior is on a mission to transform healthcare administration, making it seamless and invisible so clinicians can focus on delivering care. We've built an AI-powered platform designed by clinicians, for clinicians, to simplify administrative workflows and improve patient outcomes. By combining clinical expertise with cutting-edge technology, we're revolutionizing healthcare operations with responsible AI.

We're growing rapidly and are backed by world-class investors including Sequoia, New Enterprise Associates (NEA), and Neo along with notable angels including the founders of DeepMind, Google AI, and Inflection AI.

Anterior is improving the experience of healthcare for everyone. We're working on the cutting edge of using technology to make clinical decision-making frictionless.

We're building exciting tech for huge customers and we trust in the foundations: keep it simple, build strong foundations, and the rest will follow.

The Team:

At Anterior, we work in small, cross-functional teams — engineering, clinical, product, and customer-facing — to solve real problems for large enterprise customers. This role sits at the intersection of those teams, owning how we translate customer needs into technical solutions and get them into production.

About You:

You have a passion for doing things The Right Way. You believe Less is More. You've run programs at companies where execution actually mattered — where slipping a deadline meant real consequences, not just a status update. You've seen what good execution looks like at scale, and you've seen what happens when programs are run poorly. You care about the fundamentals: clear scope, real dependencies, honest timelines.

You're technical enough to earn the respect of the engineers you work with. You can read a system design doc, understand a deployment pipeline, and ask the right questions in a postmortem. You don't need to write production code, but you need to understand the systems well enough to know where the real risks are.

You may be coming from a TPM, solutions architect, or technical deployment role at a scale-up, big tech, or a high-growth startup. What matters is that you've owned complex technical programs end-to-end — scoping solutions for enterprise customers, coordinating across teams, and shipping.

https://電.anterior.app/ — if you find something here that resonates, mention it in your application.

About the Role:

The Technical Solutions Architect at Anterior will own how we design and deliver solutions for our enterprise customers — from scoping integrations through to successful deployment. You'll work across engineering, product, clinical, and customer-facing teams to drive programs that span our platform, integrations, and enterprise deployments.

  • Designing and driving end-to-end solutions for enterprise customers, from scoping through delivery

  • Coordinating across engineering, product, clinical, and customer teams to keep enterprise integrations and deployments on track

  • Identifying dependencies, risks, and blockers early — and resolving them before they become problems

  • Building lightweight program management processes that fit a fast-moving ~10-person engineering team (not importing big-company overhead)

  • Managing the cadence of engineering planning, tracking progress, and ensuring the team has clear priorities

  • Working directly with enterprise customers' technical teams to architect integration solutions, scope timelines, and define requirements

  • Driving incident response coordination and postmortem follow-through

  • Contributing to how we scale our deployment and solutions practice — processes, tooling, and playbooks that work for 5 customers and will still work for 50

Tech

You don't need to be a developer, but familiarity with the following will make you effective from day one:

  • Our stack: Python, Go, TypeScript, React

  • Infra: AWS, Nix (our build system), CDKTF

  • Concepts: distributed systems, API integrations, CI/CD pipelines, enterprise deployment patterns

What's in it for you

This is an opportunity to join a company at its major inflection point, using cutting-edge technology to have a meaningful impact in healthcare. You'll work with a world-class team, hold significant responsibility, and have the opportunity to learn and grow rapidly.

We take pride in our strong apprenticeship and coaching culture. We expect our early hires to become leaders both within our company and across the industry. You'll be given a lot of responsibility and the opportunity for rapid growth.

Benefits

  • Equity package

  • Competitive, top-of-market salary

  • 100% covered health, dental, and vision insurance

  • Catered lunches and a stocked kitchen

  • Commuter benefits

  • Company laptop along w/ tools you need to succeed

  • Learning & development budget

  • Team-building events

  • Flexible PTO

WHAT'S IN IT FOR YOU

This is an opportunity to join a company at its major inflection point, using cutting-edge technology to have a meaningful impact in healthcare. You'll work with a world-class team, hold significant responsibility, and have the opportunity to learn and grow rapidly.

We take pride in our strong apprenticeship and coaching culture. We expect our early hires to become leaders both within our company and across the industry. You'll be given a lot of responsibility and the opportunity for rapid growth.




BENEFITS

 - Equity package

 - Competitive, top-of-market salary

 - 100% covered health, dental, and vision insurance

 - Catered lunches and a stocked kitchen

 - Commuter benefits

 - Company laptop along w/ tools you need to succeed

 - Learning & development budget

 - Team-building events

 - Flexible PTO

jobs.title: Member of Technical Staff (Frontend)
jobs.descriptionHtml:
About Anterior

Anterior is on a mission to transform healthcare administration, making it seamless and invisible so clinicians can focus on delivering care. We’ve built an AI-powered platform designed by clinicians, for clinicians, to simplify administrative workflows and improve patient outcomes. By combining clinical expertise with cutting-edge technology, we’re revolutionizing healthcare operations with responsible AI.

We're growing rapidly and are backed by world-class investors including Sequoia, New Enterprise Associates (NEA), and Neo along with notable angels including the founders of DeepMind, Google AI, and Inflection AI.

Anterior is improving the experience of healthcare for everyone. We're working on the cutting edge of using technology to make clinical decision-making frictionless.

We're building exciting tech for huge customers and we trust in the foundations: keep it simple, build strong foundations, and the rest will follow.

The Team

At Anterior, engineers share a strong "sense of product" and solve meaningful problems that simplify healthcare workflows. We collaborate in multidisciplinary teams to deliver secure, scalable, and user-focused solutions guided by best practices and modular design.

We value diverse experiences and skill sets, continually expanding our team to tackle modern healthcare’s challenges with creativity and precision.

About You

You have a passion for doing things The Right Way, you believe Less is More, you want to focus on the foundations of system design and architecture (pacelc, consistency guarantees), you care as much about the code you do not write, you’re tired of the hype treadmill and care about the fundamentals. You have battle scars from over-engineered and underperforming slop, and you care about writing code that lasts.

Your strength is frontend — you've built complex, performant UIs (not just React components — state management, performance, accessibility) — but you can comfortably work across the stack. You understand and engage with backend complexity, and you're excited about working in a backend-heavy environment rather than resistant to it. You may be coming from a product-focused engineering role at a startup or scale-up where you owned UI/UX end-to-end.

You have a few years of strong experience under your belt and you're ready to take on more. You've shipped real features, worked in production codebases, and you're looking for a place where you'll grow fast with real ownership — not a place where you'll be writing tickets for someone else to build.

Come share your proudest firefighting story with us in an interview, bring your GitHub, and let’s see if we’re a good fit.

https://電.anterior.app/ — if you find something here that resonates, mention it in your application.

About the Role

Members of Technical Staff at Anterior own problems end-to-end — from system design through to production. You'll work across the stack, owning the user-facing experience while engaging deeply with the backend systems that power it. A day's work may include:

  • Building and owning the frontend experiences that clinicians and customers interact with daily — React and HTMX

  • Working across the stack — from frontend through to backend services — to ship complete solutions

  • Working directly with customers to understand real-world healthcare workflows, scope integrations, and translate requirements into product specs

  • Designing and building backend services, APIs, and data pipelines that power our AI platform

  • Writing evals to identify weaknesses and drive improvements in our LLM-powered systems

  • Building internal tooling to automate workflows and increase team leverage

  • Analyzing request logs, debugging event streams, and improving system reliability

  • Contributing to Anterior's SDKs, developer documentation, and platform architecture

Tech

  • Langs: Python / Go / TS, React (and HTMX if you can help!)

  • Infra: Nix, AWS (currently on CDKTF — actively looking to migrate, bonus points if you've done this before)

What’s in it for you

This is an opportunity to join a company at its major inflection point, using cutting-edge technology to have a meaningful impact in healthcare. You'll work with a world-class team, hold significant responsibility, and have the opportunity to learn and grow rapidly.

We take pride in our strong apprenticeship and coaching culture. We expect our early hires to become leaders both within our company and across the industry. You’ll be given a lot of responsibility and the opportunity for rapid growth.

Benefits:

  • Equity package

  • Competitive, top-of-market salary

  • 100% covered health, dental, and vision insurance

  • Catered lunches and a stocked kitchen

  • Commuter benefits

  • Company laptop along w/ tools you need to succeed

  • Learning & development budget

  • Team-building events

  • Flexible PTO

You have a few years of strong experience under your belt and you're ready to take on more. You've shipped real features, worked in production codebases, and you're looking for a place where you'll grow fast with real ownership — not a place where you'll be writing tickets for someone else to build.

Come share your proudest firefighting story with us in an interview, bring your GitHub, and let’s see if we’re a good fit.

https://電.anterior.app/ https://xn--xp5a.anterior.app — if you find something here that resonates, mention it in your application.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Members of Technical Staff at Anterior own problems end-to-end — from system design through to production. You'll work across the stack, owning the user-facing experience while engaging deeply with the backend systems that power it. A day's work may include:

 - Building and owning the frontend experiences that clinicians and customers interact with daily — React and HTMX

 - Working across the stack — from frontend through to backend services — to ship complete solutions

 - Working directly with customers to understand real-world healthcare workflows, scope integrations, and translate requirements into product specs

 - Designing and building backend services, APIs, and data pipelines that power our AI platform

 - Writing evals to identify weaknesses and drive improvements in our LLM-powered systems

 - Building internal tooling to automate workflows and increase team leverage

 - Analyzing request logs, debugging event streams, and improving system reliability

 - Contributing to Anterior's SDKs, developer documentation, and platform architecture




TECH

 - Langs: Python / Go / TS, React (and HTMX if you can help!)

 - Infra: Nix, AWS (currently on CDKTF — actively looking to migrate, bonus points if you've done this before)




WHAT’S IN IT FOR YOU

This is an opportunity to join a company at its major inflection point, using cutting-edge technology to have a meaningful impact in healthcare. You'll work with a world-class team, hold significant responsibility, and have the opportunity to learn and grow rapidly.

We take pride in our strong apprenticeship and coaching culture. We expect our early hires to become leaders both within our company and across the industry. You’ll be given a lot of responsibility and the opportunity for rapid growth.




BENEFITS:

 - Equity package

 - Competitive, top-of-market salary

 - 100% covered health, dental, and vision insurance

 - Catered lunches and a stocked kitchen

 - Commuter benefits

 - Company laptop along w/ tools you need to succeed

 - Learning & development budget

 - Team-building events

 - Flexible PTO

jobs.title: Clinical AI Intern
jobs.descriptionHtml:
About Anterior

Anterior is on a mission to transform healthcare administration, making it seamless and invisible so clinicians can focus on delivering care. We've built an AI-powered platform designed by clinicians, for clinicians, to simplify administrative workflows and improve patient outcomes. By combining clinical expertise with cutting-edge technology, we're revolutionizing healthcare operations with responsible AI.

We're growing rapidly and are backed by world-class investors including Sequoia, New Enterprise Associates (NEA), and Neo along with notable angels including the founders of DeepMind, Google AI, and Inflection AI.

Anterior is improving the experience of healthcare for everyone. We're working on the cutting edge of using technology to make clinical decision-making frictionless.

We're building exciting tech for huge customers and we trust in the foundations: keep it simple, build strong foundations, and the rest will follow.

The Role

We are looking for a Clinical AI Intern to join our clinical team. You will use Anterior's proprietary technology to transform a high volume of clinical policies into structured reasoning representations, validate their quality, and prepare them for production deployment.

You will work closely with our clinical team, driving the end-to-end process from raw clinical guidelines to deployment-ready structured logic. Your work will directly support live product rollouts.

What You Will Do

  • Use our proprietary technology to transform clinical guidelines into structured reasoning graphs, and ensure structural quality

  • Validate that structured policy representations faithfully capture the intended clinical logic

  • Configure and prepare policy representations for production deployment

  • Surface edge cases, ambiguities, and logic gaps across policy representations

  • Contribute to the broader policy transformation pipeline, including working with engineering tools and evolving technical workflows

  • Collaborate with the team on a potential publication related to policy digitization methods and outcomes

Who You Are

  • Graduate student in biomedical informatics, health informatics, clinical informatics, or a related field; or an MD, MD/PhD, or PhD student with clinical knowledge

  • Comfortable reading and reasoning about clinical policy content

  • Strong attention to detail, particularly for QA and validation work

  • Technically fluent enough to learn and run scripts, and to work within structured data and logic representations

  • Comfortable with ambiguity and evolving processes

  • Interest in AI, clinical decision support, or health policy systems

Bonus points for:

  • Coursework or experience in knowledge representation, knowledge engineering, or structured clinical logic

  • HIPAA training or clinical research experience

  • Familiarity with healthcare standards or terminologies (e.g., CPT, ICD, clinical guidelines)
